We can visualize the bootstrap distribution
to assess the sampling distribution of our estimate:

::: {#fig-bootstrap-dist}

```{r}
#| code-fold: true
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 4

tibble(boot_rd = boot_estimates) |>
  ggplot() +
  aes(x = boot_rd) +
  geom_histogram(
    bins = 50,
    fill = "steelblue",
    alpha = 0.7,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = marginal_rd_est,
    color = "red",
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = ci_lower,
    color = "darkgreen",
    linetype = "dotted",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = ci_upper,
    color = "darkgreen",
    linetype = "dotted",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Bootstrap Marginal Risk Difference",
    y = "Frequency",
    title = "Bootstrap Distribution of Causal Marginal Risk Difference"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

Bootstrap distribution of the marginal risk difference
(Type A vs Type B behavioral pattern, adjusted for age).
Red dashed line: point estimate.
Green dotted lines: 95% CI bounds.

:::
